ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Indomie instant noodle dish with egg and salad; 'noodle dish' (FOODON_00002187) used as the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00GJ (Noodles, cooked) used as the closest defensible anchor; no specific code exists for instant noodle dish with egg and salad accompaniments.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this specific instant noodle dish preparation.

serving.natural_language_portion: One ala carte plate of Indomie Jumbo with egg and salad typically weighs approximately 350 g, comprising a jumbo portion of instant noodles with seasoning, one sunny-side-up egg, and a small side salad.

nutrient_source.title: Indomie Jumbo With Egg & Salad (Ala Carte) — HPB Singapore Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for 'Indomie Jumbo With Egg & Salad (Ala Carte)' (cr_id G00010432). All per-100g values are as reported. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central data for comparable instant noodle dishes with egg (USDA SR Legacy #18069 and #01129) as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this entry; monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and water are set to -1 as not reported by HPB for this entry. Trans fat is estimated from published literature on palm-oil-fried instant noodles.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Indomie Jumbo with Egg and Salad as a composite dish. GI of 73 is assigned based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for instant noodles (white wheat, boiled), which reports GI ~73 (glucose reference). The egg and salad components contribute negligible carbohydrate and do not materially alter the composite GI. Glycemic load calculated as: GI (73) × available carbohydrate per serving (350 g × 5.9 g/100g = 20.65 g) / 100 = 15.1.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 810 mg/100 g — far exceeds the 600 mg/100 g upper threshold; potassium at 64 mg/100 g provides negligible counter-balance
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this dish or, at minimum, use only half the seasoning sachet and avoid additional condiments such as soy sauce or chilli sauce, as sodium at 810 mg/100 g is critically elevated and a single serving can deliver over 2,800 mg sodium.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High GI instant noodles (GI ~73) combined with very high fat content (44.5 g/100 g) worsens postprandial glucose response and insulin resistance
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit this dish because the refined-flour instant noodles carry a high glycemic index (~73), and the very high fat content (predominantly saturated and likely trans fat from palm-oil frying) further impairs insulin sensitivity and elevates postprandial blood glucose.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat ~18 g/100 g and trans fat ~0.5 g/100 g combined with sodium 810 mg/100 g present compounded cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid this dish due to the combination of very high saturated fat (~18 g/100 g), estimated trans fat (~0.5 g/100 g from palm-oil-fried noodles), and critically elevated sodium (810 mg/100 g), all of which independently and synergistically raise LDL cholesterol, promote arterial inflammation, and elevate blood pressure.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Estimated trans fat (~0.5 g/100 g) from palm-oil-fried noodles is strongly pro-inflammatory; high saturated fat and sodium further worsen joint inflammation
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this dish as the estimated trans fat from palm-oil-fried instant noodles is a strong pro-inflammatory trigger, and the high saturated fat and sodium content further exacerbate joint inflammation and tissue swelling.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Indomie Jumbo with Egg and Salad (Ala Carte) is a popular hawker and food-court dish served in Singapore, featuring a jumbo-sized portion of Indomie instant noodles — Indonesia's iconic brand — prepared with the included seasoning sachet (typically containing salt, flavour enhancers, chilli powder, and palm-oil-based flavouring), topped with a sunny-side-up fried egg, and accompanied by a small side salad of fresh vegetables such as lettuce, tomato, and cucumber. The noodles are boiled and then tossed with the seasoning, resulting in a savoury, mildly spicy, oily dish with a soft-chewy noodle texture. The egg adds richness and protein, while the salad provides a fresh, crunchy contrast. Nutritionally, this dish is characterised by very high fat content (44.5 g/100 g), predominantly from the palm-oil-fried noodle cake and seasoning oil, with estimated saturated fat around 18 g/100 g and possible trans fat from the frying process. Sodium is critically elevated at 810 mg/100 g, making this dish unsuitable for individuals managing hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or Type 2 Diabetes. The glycemic index of the noodle base is approximately 73 (high), and a typical 350 g serving delivers a glycemic load of approximately 15. Despite its widespread popularity as a comfort food and budget meal, this dish presents significant clinical concerns across multiple chronic disease categories and should be consumed only occasionally and in moderation.
common_user_queries: Instant noodles with egg glycemic index

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010432, confidence 0.95), which is the primary approved source for Singapore food records. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central analogous instant noodle and egg data; trans fat estimated from published literature on palm-oil-fried instant noodles. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database entry for instant noodles (white wheat, boiled). All estimations are documented.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (18.0 g/100 g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for comparable instant noodle dishes with egg, as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this entry. 2. Trans fat (0.5 g/100 g) is estimated from published literature on palm-oil-fried instant noodles; this is an approximation and may vary by product batch. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation and moisture data for this specific HPB composite dish entry are not available in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, and selenium_mcg are set to -1: micronutrient detail for this composite dish is not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for G00010432. 4. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 as reported by HPB; this is plausible given the refined-flour noodle base and minimal salad component in the per-100g composite. 5. GI of 73 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database data for instant noodles (white wheat, boiled); no direct GI measurement exists for this specific composite dish. 6. This dish is classified as ultra-processed due to the instant noodle base with seasoning sachet containing flavour enhancers and palm oil. 7. Halal status is based on Indomie's general halal certification in Singapore; consumers should verify current certification status.
